articles.title: Stock market strategy: Buy on dips or sell on rallies? Here’s what experts recommend
articles.summary: Stock market strategy: Rather than focusing solely on Nifty 50 stocks, investors should consider companies outside the index that are delivering robust earnings growth. Attractive opportunities currently exist across large-cap, mid-cap and small-cap segments, analyst said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: NBFC stock Aptus Value Housing Finance climbs over 3%; Citi sees over 30% upside potential
articles.summary: Shares of Aptus Value Housing Finance have been enjoying healthy buying interest of late. They have jumped over 35% in the last three months. So far in June, the stock is up more than 3%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty IT index crashes 28% YTD. Buy or wait, as renewed US-Iran tension may jeopardise crude oil supply again?
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks have declined 28.71% YTD, outpacing the Nifty 50's 8.32% drop due to concerns over slowing global spending. Analysts suggest a tentative recovery, influenced by delayed client decisions and geopolitical tensions, as traditional growth models face disruption from AI.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Jivial Industries IPO allotment likely to be out today. Here's step-by-step guide on how to check status
articles.summary: The IPO has been priced at ₹196 per share and consists of a fresh issue worth ₹26.65 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of ₹5.34 crore, taking the total issue size to ₹31.99 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can Astral's demerger catalyze chemicals growth?
articles.summary: Astral's chemicals demerger sharpens business focus, but investors remain unconvinced. The key question is whether the spin-off can revive margins and justify the stock's rich valuation.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Radico Khaitan, Allied Blenders shares get Jefferies’ ‘Buy’ rating; up to 25% upside seen; Should you buy?
articles.summary: Jefferies noted that Indian alcoholic beverage stocks have significantly outperformed the broader staples sector during FY23–FY26, delivering an earnings per share (EPS) CAGR of over 30%, compared with mid-to-high single-digit growth for staples.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The trade-off in the Persistent-Nagarro deal is hard to ignore
articles.summary: Persistent Systems' share price fell 10% to a 52-week low after the IT company announced the acquisition of Munich-based Naggaro SE—its largest ever at an enterprise valuation of €1.27 billion.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, how to check status
articles.summary: Advit Jewels IPO is commanding a grey market premium (GMP) of ₹51 today. The allotment is likely to be finalised on Monday, 29 June.

articles.title: Astral share price slips 10% after demerger announcement. Should you buy or sell?
articles.summary: Astral demerger ratio is set at 1:1. This means subject to the necessary approvals, Astral shareholders will receive shares of Astral Chemie in proportion to their existing shareholding (in ratio of 1:1).

articles.summary: Indian equity benchmarks, Nifty 50 and Sensex, declined due to geopolitical tensions impacting investor sentiment. The BSE Sensex fell over 400 pts, and Nifty 50 dropped around 115 pts. Upcoming domestic macroeconomic indicators will guide investors amid concerns over US-Iran military exchanges.

Upcoming IPOs, including CSM Technologies, Aastha Spintex, and Knack Packaging, maintain market momentum despite volatility.

articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares witnessed some profit booking as its chief executive officer (CEO), Ashok Vaswani, said he will step down at the end of his current term on 31 December 2026.

articles.summary: Transrail Lighting share price has risen 9% in one month and has gained 10% in three months. The smallcap stock has fallen 8% in six months, while it has dropped nearly 21% in one year.

articles.summary: Gold and silver rates declined on MCX as fresh escalations in US-Iran tensions drove oil prices up. MCX gold August futures were 0.40% down at ₹1,43,583 per 10 grams, while MCX silver September contracts were 0.26% down at ₹2,22,899 per kg around 9:15 am.

articles.title: Rupee opens 5 paise higher at 94.35 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened to 94.35 against the US dollar, supported by lower oil prices despite potential global risks. Analysts suggest the rupee may trade within the 94-95 range, with strong capital inflows providing some buffer against a firm US dollar.

articles.title: US dollar set to log highest monthly gain in one-year. US-Iran war, job data in focus

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 29 June
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a tepid start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,089 level, a discount of nearly 13 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
articles.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
articles.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt eases Quality Control Order rules: Which industry segments are likely to get relief?
articles.summary: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Will water shortages derail India’s AI Data Centre boom?
articles.summary: India's ambition to become a leading AI data centre hub brings unprecedented investments, but it also raises crucial questions about sustainability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
articles.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
articles.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
articles.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH
